mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate above a 0.3 percentage point threshold over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate profits, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion that boosts corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects capacity utilization nearing limits, which triggers inflationary pressures and anticipated monetary tightening that suppresses S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration in late-cycle regimes does not precede negative 20-day equity returns, or the signal loses predictive power when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls consistently correlate with expanding economic output and higher corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of the broader cycle phase.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows a negative or null correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 2.5% year-over-year growth threshold, anticipated central bank rate hikes increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 1% over 20 days in > 70% of instances where inflation crosses the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and total payrolls indicate synchronized consumer and labor market strength that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The retail-payroll synchronization metric yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a combination of low federal funds rates and falling unemployment signals effective monetary transmission that rapidly restores corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint low-rate and falling unemployment signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in > 55% of post-shock recovery periods.
